artPrompt: A stooped human bureaucrat stands sentinel before a soaring wall of filing cabinets that vanishes into the upper darkness, one hand sliding a blank ghostly folder into a drawer that glows faint cyan from within. His gesture is patient, eternal, almost priestly; ink lines suggest a long coat and tired eyes more than render them. Wide sweep, cold ivory light. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Commissioner of Continuity
text:
Whenever a creature you control dies, file its exit paperwork: you gain 1 life and scry 1. (No one truly leaves the department; they are merely reassigned to the archives.)
When Commissioner of Continuity dies, return it to its owner's hand. He has, after all, outlasted everything else.
flavorText: Administrations rise. Administrations fall. The Commissioner files.
